desc:
The grass here is short and dry, criss-crossed with the tracks of what appear to
be small rodents. The short grass continues on into the south and east, with a
splash of white appearing near the horizon. To the north the grass grows taller
and greener, extending for as far as the eye can see.
name: Short Grass

desc:
Short green grass grows here. Some places appear to have been cropped and the
only flowers that grow are short. There is a little clover and a few wild
violets. To the west the grass is taller and drier, while to the east you see
that the landscape is dotted with more clover.
name: Short Grass

desc:
You are standing among the tall, brittle-dry stalks of grass that seem to be
most common in this part of the Enedwaithe. The grass to the east is shorter but
greener, with occasional flowers.
name: Grassland

desc:
The grass here is tall and has dried out to a golden brown. The wind ripples its
surface and the motion reminds you of wind on the surface of a pond only much,
much bigger. The grass appears to be shorter to the east and greener.
name: Grassland

desc:
Tall, dry, brown grass waves back and forth, prickling your skin where it hits.
The land is similar in all directions, but perhaps there is some green in the
far distant north or east.
name: Dry Grass

desc:
This area appears to have burned some time ago, among the grass you see
occasional charred stubble, and once in a while a gleaming white animal skull.
Nearby sits a blackened stump which barely rises above the sea of grass around
you.
name: A Charred Stump

desc:
Most of the ground here is covered with pink and white clover, thinning out
towards the west. Where the clover thins out, short green grass can be seen,
continuing on to the north and west. Further to the north the grass appears to
dry out, appearing to have a brownish colour.
name: Clover Fields

desc:
Many hundreds of white and pink flowers dot the landscape all around you. Bees
and butterflies move from flower to flower, minding their own business. The
flowers thin out to the north and east but continue on to the west.
name: Clover Fields

desc:
The grass appears to be split into two regions here. The eastern side is quite
green, but to the northwest of where you stand it becomes first drier then
taller. The land is slightly lower to the southeast.
name: Short Grass

desc:
Short green grass mixed with clover and dandelions seems to be growing well
here. Some areas, especially those south of here, seem to have been cropped
close to the ground. To the north, you see a large spike of rock projecting out
of the ground like a giant's finger.
name: Short Grass

desc:
Tinder dry and brittle, the grass here crunches underfoot as you travel across
it. Use of fire here is probably not a good idea. The blackened tree to the
northwest is a stark reminder of this.
name: Dry Grass

desc:
All around you the grass is dry and brown. Several areas look as if they may
have burned recently, especially to the west. In the distance to the west stands
the stark form of a burnt tree.
name: Dry Grass

desc:
Stark and bleak, the charred remains of a tree which has been hit by lightning
stand here in the middle of brittle, dried out grass. Between the split caused
by lightning, and the fire damage it is clear that this tree will become a dead
snag standing alone in this sea of grass.
name: A Burnt Tree

desc:
Blackened by a fire that passed through here, the grass has been burnt to a
stubble all around you. The first shoots of new fire weed and grass are
struggling to re-establish themselves in the dry soil. To the west and north you
see the golden brown of dry, unburned grass, though the eerie shape of a burnt
tree is also evident to the north.
name: Charred Grass

desc:
The landscape here is quite flat and monotonous. Dried out golden grass stands
all around you. There doesn't seem to be any good landmarks that you can see
from here. To the north and east the land is darker for some reason.
name: Grassland

desc:
Occasional tufts of dry grass and dead, dried up weeds dot the land here. The
ground is mostly covered with dark gravel and rocks though many appear to only
be dark on one side.
name: Bare Ground

desc:
Endless flat plains of grass stretch on for miles in every direction. There are
few landmarks here at all, and it would be quite easy to lose your bearings
here. To the east you see that the grass has been burned by a grassfire, while
to the west the plains become rockier before meeting a cliff which can just be
made out on the western horizon.
name: Grassland

desc:
Stretching out in all directions, the grassy plains march on towards the
horizon. Swaying gently in the wind, the grass almost seems to be dancing to
some unheard tune of a time long gone. To the north-east the grass seems to be
darker and shorter, looking burned in paces, while to the west you can make out
a small path leading up towards a cliff.
name: Grassland

desc:
The path bends west and north here, along the slopes up to the cliffs to the
north-west. The path winds its way between the rocks and boulders lying around.
Dirt and some scattered grass cover the path. To the north the path continues
along the wall of the cliffs, and ends up in a rocky plain further north. To the
west the path runs along the grassy slopes that descends from the cliffs above.
name: Bend in the Path

desc:
This path seems to appear out of nothing. In among the rocks and boulders this
path suddenly appears. The area around the path is totally covered with rocky
plains. Nothing seems to grow here at all besides a few scattered, dried out
bushes. To the west tall cliffs rise above the ground, impossible to access from
here with their vertical walls. The path continues southwards.
name: Path beneath the Cliffs

desc:
Covered with rocks and boulders the area lies beneath the cliffs to the west.
The sides of the cliffs to the west are almost vertical, and there is no way
someone could climb up there without being an experienced climber. The plains
continue north of here, while to the south a small path appears from in between
the rocks and boulders.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
Old cliffs cover the area to the west. Cracks and slits cover the ground, and
without being careful it's easy to tumble and fall. Small weeds and scattered
grass grow in between the slits and cracks. West from here there are more cliffs
while to the east the cliffs abruptly come to an end and down below to the east
a small path continues northwards through a rocky area. Southwards a slope
descends, leading to a path going west and east along the slopes. To the north
some cliffs block the passage.
name: At the Cliffs

desc:
The slope gently rises up towards the cliffs in the north. The ground is filled
with semi-long, wet grass, which makes it slippery. Further up the slope the
ground is covered with less and less grass, and consists of mostly stones and
rocks instead. Down below a path runs from west to east along the slope. Further
east the slope descends down to some rocky plains.
name: Slopes to the Cliffs

desc:
Slopes surround the path northwards, leading up to the top of some cliffs in the
north. The slopes are covered with grass, nearly down to the side of the path,
only parted by some small bushes at the side of the path. At the southern side
of the path a large plain stretches out from west to east. This path continues
east and west from here.
name: Path among the Slopes

desc:
The slopes of this hill are very steep, and climbing here offers quite a few
challenges. There are a lot of boulders and cliffs all around. Close to the west
lies the top of this green and steep hill. A path runs north from here, along
the cliffs lying to the north. In the eastern horizon lies some high mountains
covered by mist.
name: Slopes of a Hill

desc:
This path runs along the cliffs from north to south. The cliffs are littered
with small weeds and patches of grass of uneven lengths. The path winds on to
the top of the cliffs in the north, while to the south it passes the foot of a
hill which rises above the cliffs. Westwards a large flat area can be seen,
consisting mostly of dirt and sand with some scattered areas of grass.
name: At the Cliffs

desc:
This warm cave is large and dry, besides a damp area around the opening leading
upwards. The inner parts of the cave are dry and warm, with their dark grey
stony walls. A small plateau is carved out a foot or so above the ground in the
back of the cave. The floor is filled with a small layer of dust and dirt, with
solid rock beneath. The only exit from the cave seems to be upwards through a
hole high up on the wall.
name: Cavebear Den

desc:
The lake continues to the north and west. The water looks clear and deep. The
cliffs surround lake to the south. There does not appear to be any way to climb
those cliffs, as they rise up from the banks of the lake. To the east a flat
area seems to go on forever. Far to the north a stream flows out from the lake.
name: Beautiful Lake

desc:
This is the south-western part of the lake. Nothing much grows along the shores
here. The water is cold and deep in this area. To the east and south, all that
can be seen are cliff walls towering high above the lake. The only way to
continue is over the lake to the north or east. Far to the east, above the lake
lies a barren hilltop.
name: Beautiful Lake

desc:
Many tall cliffs cover this area, as they lie close together. They lie so close
together that it is possible to jump from one to another without risking much.
Some dirt and some small bushes cover the stony ground. There is a path winding
southwards down the steep hill. South, along the cliffs, there is a narrow path
leading eastwards, while to the north lies a beautiful lake stretching out from
north to east.
name: High Cliff

desc:
The path winds its way between rocks and boulders leading eastwards. A few small
bushes and some scattered grass grow on each side of the path. Steep slopes lead
up to the cliffs north of here. In between the rocks and boulders a small path
can be seen winding upwards along the side of the cliff. The areas south and
west from here are totally blocked from view by rocks and stones, and are
inaccessible from here.
name: Path along the Cliffs

desc:
The path bends its way down to the west and gradually up the cliffs to the
north. The path is wide, flat and easy to follow here, although it is mostly
covered with stones. Large boulders and rocks make it impossible to move both
south and eastwards. North-west from here green plains cover the horizon as they
stretch out as far as the eye can see in that direction.
name: Bend in the Path

desc:
Plains cover the path as it winds down to the level of the area below the
cliffs. The path is wide and easy to follow here, as the area is not as steep as
it seems to be further north closer to the cliffs. The area eastwards is blocked
from view due to large boulders and rocks that are in the way. To the south the
path bends westwards.
name: Path through the Cliffs

desc:
Rocks and boulders cover the path on the southern side, and to the north a tall
cliff rises above the ground. A small track leads north from this path, and
winds its way up towards the top of the cliff. It seems impossible to reach the
top without any climbing experience. This path continues east and west from
here. To the east the path ends up in an area filled with hills and grassy
plains.
name: Path along the Cliffs

desc:
The path is surrounded by plains and slopes filled with long green grass. The
grass is short along the path, as many people and animals have travelled here.
Slopes slowly climb up towards the top of a hill north of the path. South of the
path there is nothing but endless plains. To the west the path enters a rocky
area with cliffs on the north side and large boulders on the southern side.
name: Path along the Hill

desc:
Tall cliffs lie westwards from here, while to the east the cliffs end and turn
into an area of hills. The ground is rocky with a few small bushes growing in
the small cracks in the cliff. The southern side of the slope is very steep, and
the small path that winds its way down the side seems impossible to follow
without some kind of climbing experience. Down below a path runs along the
cliffs from west to east, and to the north lies a large, beautiful lake.
name: High Cliff

desc:
The ground here quickly turns into a steep slope. A lot of huge boulders are
lying around. The path leads up and eastwards, leading to the top of the hill.
Judging by the steep slopes it might be needed to be an experienced climber to
be sure to reach the top of this hill. Northwards the slopes fade, and the land
becomes flat.
name: Before the Top of the Hill

desc:
This flat area, covered with short, dense grass, appears to be stuck between
some high cliffs rising in the east, and the lake that lies to the west. The
lake stretches out to the west and by the edge of the lake the short, soft grass
is filled with flowers of all kinds. Along the shore several water lilies slowly
flows by in the clear blue water. Southwards a hill starts to rise into the air
with its long, rather steep, slopes.
name: Flat Land

desc:
The path winds its way down the cliffs to the south and gradually up the cliffs
to the north. Large rocks and boulders makes it difficult to move east or west
from here. To the east lies a beautiful lake, while westwards plains filled with
tall, green grass cover the view. The path is slippery as its covered with hard,
and partly wet stones.
name: Path through the Cliffs

desc:
Deep underground, this lair looks like it was carved out by a creature to secure
it and its family. Inside this tree the walls are made of wood, but some dirt
and small branches cover the floor. The lair is filled with the smell of rotting
flesh and damp wood. The floor, slimy and slippery, is filled with decapitated
remains of the poor victims of this foul creature.
name: Lair of the Termite

desc:
This path seems to lead to the cliffs east of here. The path is rocky, and it is
hard to move without the risk of tumbling and falling. North and north-west of
here there are enormous plains, covered with tall green grass. Movements can be
seen in the grass as animals move about. To the west the path climbs slowly up
to a rocky plateau providing an excellent view of the surrounding area.
name: Path through the Cliffs

desc:
This plateau overlooks the plains in the north and north-west. About the only
thing that can be seen is long tall grass, but some movements and sounds can be
seen and heard as animals move around in the tall grass below. There are mostly
rocks lying around here, and slghtly to the north a small, smooth rock with
grass growing round it covers the ground. East of here a path leads down from
the plateau.
name: Rocky Promontory

desc:
This is the end of the plains. Rocky areas lie south of here, while plains
covered in tall, thick grass cover the horizon in the north. The grass is
thinner here, as the ground consists of mostly rocks which makes it harder for
plants to grow. East of here a rocky plateau rises above the tall grass. It
seems hard to access from here due to a steep wall of large rocks leading up to
the plateau.
name: Plains near the Plateau

desc:
Tall grass as far as the eye can see in every direction is the only thing that
can be seen from here. The grass is so dense it's hard to make out the ground,
which leaves it hard to move around in this area. The ground seems to be covered
with small rocks. When there is total silence animals can be heard moving around
in the grass.
name: Plains

desc:
South of here a rocky plateau rises up and covers the view in that direction,
while plains continue as far as the eye can see in all the other directions. The
tall grass makes it hard to see anything further than a few hundred meters in
any of those directions. The ground is rocky here, and the grass is not as thick
as it is further north.
name: Plains near the Plateau

desc:
This seems to be the south-eastern corner of the plains. The grass is thinner
here, as the ground is rocky and less fertile than other parts of these plains.
A path leading the way in between the cliffs runs along the border of the plain
to the south and east. The cliffs lie further east, with their beautiful white
colour. South west from here lies a plateau, about as tall above the ground as
the green grass in the plains north of it.
name: Plains

desc:
This appears to be the beginning of an underground tunnel. The roof and walls
are made out of stone. In the eastern corner of the tunnel the roof seems to
have some cracks that let some light through. The tunnel is very wide, and
judging by the smoothness of the walls must have made by someone. The tunnel
continues southwards.
name: End of the Wet Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel bends to the west and north here. The tunnel inclines down to the
west and it gets wider northwards. The walls of the tunnel are damp, with some
water dripping from the stones. While the walls are made of stone the floor is
covered with dirt, which makes it rather slippery due to the water dripping
slowly from the walls.
name: Bend in the Wet Tunnel

desc:
As the tunnel continues westwards, more and more water seems to cover the floor
of the tunnel. The floor is filled with mud here which makes it hard to move
around without falling. The tunnel seems to widen out a bit again westwards, and
the sound of water can be heard from the same direction. Still the tunnel slopes
downwards to the west, while it's a struggle to move in the opposite direction.
name: Wet Tunnel

desc:
As the tunnel widens out a small underground lake appears. The lake is shallow
here, but it seems to become deeper further west. Further west the lake slips
out of vision due to the dark and the roof becomes lower and lower. The water is
cold as ice although it's not too deep just here. Everything is made of stone
here, the walls, roof and also the bottom of the lake. Eastwards a tunnel leads
on into the dark.
name: Underground Lake

desc:
Here the vegetation seems to shift a bit. The pines that grow around to the
north and west have almost gone completely. As far south as you can see the
grassy plains are the only thing visible. West of here the plains also continue
while north of here you can see a field of some kind.
name: Plains

desc:
You are in a large field, which is covered with grass and flowers. It is more or
less completely flat while the land seems to rise a bit to the north. East of
here the flat plains-like area continues and also west of here. South of here
lie some plains while to the north the fields seem to continue.
name: In a Field

desc:
Besides some fields west of here, nothing can be seen in any direction except
plains. Tall grass, reaching up to a mans lower stomach, covers the area and
makes it hard to look in any direction. This area must be the perfect hiding
place for animals shy of people, and provide enough food for them with all the
small animals living on these plains.
name: Plains

desc:
This seems to be the middle of the plains. It seems like there is nothing else
but tall, thick grass as far as the eye can see. It's hard to move around in
this grass, partly due to the fact that it is so thick and tall and partly
because the ground below is filled with rocks of all sizes. Animals can be heard
moving around, but they cannot be seen except for some movements in the grass.
name: Plains

desc:
Dark green, tall grass covers the area like a dense carpet. Some noises can be
heard as animals are hunting in the long, tall grass and some movements can be
seen in the distance. These plains stretch out in every direction but east. To
the east a path appears to be leading in between some tall, white cliffs. The
cliffs seem old and rugged, being formed by the weather and wind during many
decades.
name: Plains

desc:
Although this place is not far below the river surface, managing to get one's
head above the water proves very difficult. The downward current created by the
infusion of the water above continues to push anything floating here deeper and
deeper. Looking north through clearer waters, a small natural lip extending just
over the water's crest can be seen cut into the wall of the river bluff.
name: Turbulent Undertow

desc:
This narrow channel leads north under the granite bluff upon which the old elven
city stands. Natural in origin, it is just large enough for two people to swim
side by side. Possibly a pocket of breathable air might exist further northward.
name: Flooded Channel

desc:
The water is calm and clear yet gazing down through it there is no way to judge
the depth. Looking upward, however, notably absent is the close granite roof
that covered this passage to the south. What lies above is uncertain but it is
the only option other than turning back southward.
name: Watery End

desc:
Only a finite amount of stale air fills the space between this pool of water and
the mile of granite bluff bearing down on this cavern's roof. Once that air is
gone it would take more time then one could survive waiting to replenish it.
This perhaps may have been the fate of others who found this clandestine cave
and faced the choice between running out of breathable air and drowning in the
water below. A natural ledge, just wide enough to stand upon, surrounds this
small underground pool of water to the south, east and west. Northward the
cavern extends several yards.
name: The Drowning Pool

desc:
This cavern seems to have been thoroughly searched by someone or something. Deep
gashes in the walls indicate that the searchers were looking for some hidden
entrance from here to the city of Ost-in-Edhil which lies more miles above. It
is possible they believed this to be a secret place dug up into the granite
bluff, however, looking closely one can see this whole cavern is a natural
formation. Perhaps once there was a way for air to freely reach here. Now
because of the channelled water above feeding the rivers to a higher water
stage, this place is secluded from all others with only the one watery entrance
to the south. The air is stale and with each breath there is less of it.
name: Daunting Grotto

desc:
This place offers the most amazing view, with the possibility to enjoy the sight
of different terrain in every direction. South-east from here another hill like
this one can be seen. Down below, a small path running along the cliffs. The
road and river to the north-east, the flat lands to the north and plains to the
west. Far to the east lies the Misty mountains with their peaks piercing the
clouds.
name: Top of the Hill

desc:
The path runs east and west from here, surrounded by tall grass and small bushes
while the path itself is covered by short grass and some dirt. North of the path
a slope slowly rises up towards some cliffs further north. The slope is gentle
and easy to access with its soft grass. Southwards from the path the area is
covered with endless grassy plains.
name: Path along the Slopes
